mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic activity and expanding output, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This positive trend is persistent across the business cycle and does not require any threshold to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index rose from 106.0 in January 2018 to 107.3 in May 2018
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation between INDPRO and 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0%, monetary policy becomes restrictive, increasing discount rates and reducing economic activity, which depresses equity valuations. This threshold trigger predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 1.82% as of June 2018, near 2% threshold
failure_condition: The average 5-day return after FEDFUNDS > 2.0% is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) over a 2-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a tight labor market (UNRATE <= 4.0%) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL above 250) signals stagflationary pressures as wage and price increases force the Fed to tighten. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon, with the combined effect stronger than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% (tight) and CPIAUCSL at 250.857 (elevated) as of June 2018
failure_condition: The interaction term (product or residual) does not improve out-of-sample Rank IC over the best individual series by at least 0.02 absolute over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by FEDFUNDS above 1.5%, strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market overheating, prompting the Fed to accelerate rate hikes. This acts as bad news for equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS reached 148,912 in June 2018, consistent growth; FEDFUNDS at 1.82% above 1.5%
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, the signal from PAYEMS growth does not predict negative returns: the 60-day average return is not significantly negative (sign test p>0.05) over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) below 500,000 million dollars signals weakening consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets react to the demand shortfall.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501,971 in May 2018, close to 500,000 threshold
failure_condition: When RSAFS drops below 500,000, future 5-day returns are not significantly negative (sign test p>0.05) over a 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with declining HOUST indicates that monetary tightening is cooling the housing market, a leading indicator of economic slowdown. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as the housing downturn spreads to broader economy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST declined from 1380 in April 2018 to 1350 in May 2018; FEDFUNDS rising
failure_condition: The interaction term does not improve out-of-sample Rank IC by at least 0.02 over either individual series in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market with rising employment and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This is a persistent always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS grew from 148,000 in January 2018 to 149,000 in June 2018
failure_condition: The rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth signals sustained economic expansion, boosting corporate profits and investor confidence. This leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter (60 trading days) as the growth trajectory supports equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2018 GDP at 17371.9, up from 17255.9 in Q4 2017
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly positive (p>0.10) over the available history.
